0.Risk: Stored secrets in Git history, container images, or directly in code may expose sensitive information to unauthorized parties.
0.Measure: Regularly test for secrets in code, container images, and Git history.
0.ISO 27001:2022: VCS usage is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001 - too specific

0.Risk: Stored secrets in Git history, container images, or directly in code may expose sensitive information to unauthorized parties.
0.Measure: Regularly test for secrets in code, container images, and Git history.
0.ISO 27001:2022: VCS usage is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001 - too specific

Tools.Name: Cobertura
Tools.Description: Java tool for measuring test coverage and generating reports.

Tools.Name: Codecov
Tools.Description: Cloud service for managing and visualizing code coverage reports.

Tools.Name: Coveralls
Tools.Description: Web-based service to track and visualize code coverage trends over time.

0.Risk: Untested parts of the application may harbor vulnerabilities, reducing overall security.
0.Measure: Use coverage tools to ensure no missing application paths.

Tools.Name: ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana)
Tools.Description: Open-source suite for comprehensive logging, monitoring, and data visualization.

0.Risk: Ineffective configuration, patch, or vulnerability management may result in undetected risks.
0.Measure: Leverage coverage and control metrics to evaluate the effectiveness of security programs, including antivirus, patch management, and server configuration.

Tools.Name: Peach Fuzzer
Tools.Description: Comprehensive fuzzing framework for testing applications.

0.Risk: Specially formatted or encoded parameters, such as REST-like URLs, JSON-formatted, or base64-encoded parameters, may go untested.
0.Measure: Define special parameter encodings to ensure they are included in fuzz testing by vulnerability scanners.

Tools.Name: Tripwire
Tools.Description: Security and compliance automation tool for reducing attack surfaces.

Tools.Name: Sysinternals Suite
Tools.Description: Collection of utilities to manage, troubleshoot, and diagnose Windows systems.

Tools.Name: Lynis
Tools.Description: Open-source security auditing tool for Unix-based systems.

0.Risk: Unused components, dependencies, files, or access rights may introduce vulnerabilities.
0.Measure: Remove unnecessary components, dependencies, and access rights. Use distroless images for container environments.

0.Risk: Virtual environments using default settings may communicate with others, exposing sensitive data or services. Docker's default bridge network, for example, allows communication between all containers on a host.
0.Measure: Regulate and control communication between virtual environments to ensure isolation.
0.ISO 27001:2022: Virtual environments are not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

0.Risk: Failure to apply standard hardening practices for cloud environments may lead to vulnerabilities.
0.Measure: Use tools to automate the testing of virtual environment configurations.
0.ISO 27001:2022: System hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

2.Description: Baseline hardening establishes a secure configuration baseline for systems and infrastructure components, ensuring adherence to organizational security standards and reducing vulnerabilities. Tools like Ansible, Puppet, and Chef enforce baseline configurations within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Automates baseline security configurations across multiple systems.

Tools.Name: Puppet
Tools.Description: Applies baseline security standards using configuration management.

Tools.Name: Chef
Tools.Description: Manages and enforces baseline configurations within CI/CD workflows.

2.Risk: Default configurations in cluster environments pose significant security risks.
2.Measure: Implement hardening practices such as the 'CIS Kubernetes Benchmark for Security'.

2.Description: Baseline hardening establishes a secure configuration baseline for systems and infrastructure components, ensuring adherence to organizational security standards and reducing vulnerabilities. Tools like Ansible, Puppet, and Chef enforce baseline configurations within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Automates baseline security configurations across multiple systems.

Tools.Name: Puppet
Tools.Description: Applies security standards using configuration management.

Tools.Name: Chef
Tools.Description: Manages and enforces security configurations within CI/CD workflows.

2.Risk: Default configurations in cluster environments pose significant security risks.
2.Measure: Implement hardening practices such as the 'CIS Kubernetes Benchmark for Security'.

2.Description: Rewarding good communication involves recognizing and incentivizing effective collaboration and information sharing among team members. This practice fosters a culture of transparency, proactive security practices, and enhanced team cohesion. Tools like Slack and Microsoft Teams support communication, while platforms like Bonusly manage rewards and recognition.
2.Risk: Lack of incentives may reduce employee engagement in security practices.
2.Measure: Encourage cross-organizational support through gamification and rewards such as T-shirts, mugs, gift cards, and other recognition methods.
2.ISO 27001:2017: A7.2.3 addresses misconduct processes but lacks incentives for good behavior.

2.Description: Implementing role-based access controls (RBAC) ensures users have appropriate permissions based on their roles, enhancing security by restricting access to sensitive functionalities. Tools like Auth0, Okta, and AWS IAM automate the enforcement of RBAC in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Auth0
Tools.Description: Identity management platform providing robust RBAC features.

Tools.Name: Okta
Tools.Description: Access management service supporting role-based user permissions.

Tools.Name: AWS IAM
Tools.Description: AWS service for managing roles and enforcing RBAC.

2.Risk: Not performing authentication during scans may leave parts of the service untested.
2.Measure: Integrate authentication for all roles used in the service to ensure comprehensive coverage.

2.Description: Reducing the attack surface involves disabling unnecessary services, removing unused software, and limiting exposed interfaces to minimize vulnerabilities.

Tools.Name: Microsoft Baseline Security Analyzer (MBSA)
Tools.Description: Scans Windows systems for security misconfigurations and missing updates.

Tools.Name: OpenVAS
Tools.Description: Open-source vulnerability scanning and management tool.

Tools.Name: Nessus
Tools.Description: Comprehensive tool for vulnerability scanning.

Tools.Name: Qualys
Tools.Description: Cloud-based solution for security and compliance, including attack surface reduction.

2.Risk: Unnecessary services and software increase the potential attack surface.
2.Measure: Identify and disable unused services, software, and interfaces.

2.Activity: Applications Are Running in Virtualized Environments
2.Description: Deploying applications in virtualized environments enhances scalability, flexibility, and security. Tools like Docker, Kubernetes, and VMware vSphere enable efficient resource utilization and application isolation.

Tools.Name: Docker
Tools.Description: Platform for containerized application development and deployment.

Tools.Name: Kubernetes
Tools.Description: Container orchestration system for managing and scaling applications.

Tools.Name: VMware vSphere
Tools.Description: Virtualization platform for managing virtual machines and resources.

2.Risk: A vulnerability in one service on a server may expose other services running on the same server.
2.Measure: Ensure applications are deployed in isolated and dedicated virtualized environments.
2.ISO 27001:2022: Virtual environments are not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

2.Description: Isolating networks for virtual environments ensures that traffic between environments is segmented, preventing unauthorized access and limiting the spread of threats. Tools like AWS VPC, Azure Virtual Network, and VMware NSX enable automated creation and management of isolated networks.

Tools.Name: AWS VPC
Tools.Description: Virtual Private Cloud service for creating isolated networks in AWS.

Tools.Name: Azure Virtual Network
Tools.Description: Service for creating secure, isolated networks in Azure.

Tools.Name: VMware NSX
Tools.Description: Network virtualization platform for advanced network isolation.

2.Description: Restricting virtualized resources reduces the attack surface by ensuring that only authorized and necessary virtual environments are deployed. Tools like Terraform and Ansible enforce policies for managing virtual environments within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tool for defining, managing, and enforcing virtual environment configurations.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Automation tool for configuration management and enforcing infrastructure policies, including virtual environment restrictions.

2.Risk: Denial of service caused by a single service can affect others sharing the same resources.
2.Measure: Apply resource limits (disk, memory, CPU) to all virtual environments.
2.ISO 27001:2022: Virtual environments are not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

2.Activity: Test the Definition of Virtualized Environments
2.Description: Validating virtualized environment configurations ensures adherence to security and performance standards. Tools like Terraform, Ansible, and Packer automate the testing and validation process within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Validates virtualized environments through Infrastructure as Code (IaC), integrating with CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Automates the configuration and validation of virtual environments in CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Packer
Tools.Description: Automates creation and testing of machine images for virtual environments.

2.Risk: Misconfigured virtualized environments (e.g., insecure Dockerfiles) may lead to vulnerabilities.
2.Measure: Test the definitions of virtualized environments for insecure configurations.
2.ISO 27001:2022: System hardening and virtual environments are not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

2.Activity: Test of the Configuration of Cloud Environments
2.Description: Verifying the configuration of cloud environments ensures security, performance, and compliance with organizational policies. Tools like AWS Config, Azure Resource Manager, and Google Cloud Config Connector automate testing and validation in DevSecOps pipelines.

Tools.Name: AWS Config
Tools.Description: Monitors and evaluates AWS resource configurations to ensure compliance and security.

Tools.Name: Azure Resource Manager
Tools.Description: Manages and validates Azure resource configurations within CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Google Cloud Config Connector
Tools.Description: Manages and validates Google Cloud resource configurations through Infrastructure as Code.

2.Risk: Failure to perform standard hardening practices for cloud environments may result in vulnerabilities.
2.Measure: Test virtual environment configurations with automated tools.
2.ISO 27001:2022: System hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

2.Description: Testing cluster deployment resources ensures that resource allocations support scalability, performance, and security. Tools like Terraform, Kubernetes, and Helm automate validation and optimization of deployment configurations.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Creates, manages, and validates cluster deployment configurations using Infrastructure as Code.

Tools.Name: Kubernetes
Tools.Description: Manages cluster deployments and integrates with CI/CD pipelines for automated testing.

Tools.Name: Helm
Tools.Description: Simplifies deployment and management of Kubernetes applications.

2.Risk: Deployment configurations may contain insecure settings, exposing vulnerabilities.
2.Measure: Automate testing of deployment configurations for virtualized environments.
2.ISO 27001:2022: System hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

2.Description: Validating cloud configurations ensures they meet security and performance standards. Tools like AWS Config, Azure Resource Manager, and Google Cloud Config Connector automate testing and compliance checks within DevSecOps pipelines.

Tools.Name: AWS Config
Tools.Description: Monitors and evaluates AWS resource configurations for compliance and security.

Tools.Name: Azure Resource Manager
Tools.Description: Manages and validates Azure resource configurations in CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Google Cloud Config Connector
Tools.Description: Enables management and validation of Google Cloud configurations via Infrastructure as Code.

2.Risk: Improperly configured cloud resources may lead to vulnerabilities and inefficiencies.
2.Measure: Automate testing of cloud configurations to ensure compliance and security.
2.ISO 27001:2022: System hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

2.Activity: Each Team Has a Security Champion
2.Description: Designating a security champion for each team ensures that security best practices are consistently advocated and integrated. This fosters a security-first mindset and provides continuous oversight. Tools like Confluence and Jira can assist security champions in documentation and task tracking.

Tools.Name: Jira
Tools.Description: Project management tool for tracking security tasks and responsibilities assigned to security champions.

Tools.Name: Confluence
Tools.Description: Collaboration tool for documenting and sharing security best practices and guidelines.

2.Risk: Without a designated security champion, security responsibilities may be overlooked, and champions may lack sufficient time to allocate to their teams.
2.Measure: Ensure each team designates a dedicated individual, referred to as a 'security champion', responsible for security.
2.ISO 27001:2022: Security champions are missing in ISO 27001

2.Activity: Regular Security Training of Security Champions
2.Description: Regular training equips security champions with the latest security knowledge and best practices. This enables them to guide their teams effectively and address evolving security challenges. Platforms like Moodle and Coursera facilitate ongoing training and certification for security champions.

Tools.Name: Moodle
Tools.Description: Open-source Learning Management System for delivering security training modules to champions.

Tools.Name: Coursera
Tools.Description: Online platform offering security training courses for continuous learning.

2.Risk: Security champions may struggle to keep up with evolving security concepts and practices.
2.Measure: Provide regular and structured security training to security champions.
2.ISO 27001:2022: Security champions are missing in ISO 27001

2.Description: Requiring a Pull Request (PR) before merging ensures all code changes undergo peer review to maintain quality and security. Tools like GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket enforce PR requirements and integrate automated checks.

Tools.Name: GitHub
Tools.Description: Enforces PR requirements and integrates automated checks within CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: GitLab
Tools.Description: Mandates PRs and automates review processes in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Bitbucket
Tools.Description: Implements PR requirements and integrates security checks within CI/CD workflows.

2.Risk: Unauthorized changes to critical branches may compromise code quality and security.
2.Measure: Define policies (e.g., branch protection rules) requiring mandatory PRs and reviews before merging changes.
2.ISO 27001:2022: Peer review (four eyes principle) is not explicitly required by ISO 27001

2.Description: Visualized logging presents log data in dashboards and graphs to simplify monitoring, analysis, and trend identification. Tools like Kibana, Grafana, and Splunk provide visual representations to enhance log interpretation and response.

Tools.Name: Kibana
Tools.Description: Visualizes Elasticsearch data through interactive dashboards and graphs.

Tools.Name: Grafana
Tools.Description: Open-source platform for monitoring and visualizing log and metric data.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Comprehensive platform with built-in visualization capabilities for log and metric data.

2.Risk: Limited or no visualization of logs makes monitoring and assessments challenging, particularly for developers unfamiliar with command-line tools.
2.Measure: Implement a real-time GUI-based monitoring system for logs, enabling searches for specific attributes.

2.Description: Evaluating the time taken to apply patches ensures vulnerabilities are addressed promptly. Tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, and Puppet automate patch application tracking within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Jenkins
Tools.Description: Automates patch deployment and monitors time taken to apply patches.

Tools.Name: GitLab CI/CD
Tools.Description: Tracks and automates patch deployment processes in CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Puppet
Tools.Description: Automates configuration and patch management, tracking patch times.

2.Risk: Delayed patch application increases exposure to known vulnerabilities.
2.Measure: Evaluate and optimize patch application times based on metrics like Mean Time to Close (MTTC).

2.Description: Assessing the usage and maintenance of third-party libraries ensures they are updated and free from vulnerabilities. Tools like Snyk, Dependabot, and Renovate automate dependency monitoring and updates within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Snyk
Tools.Description: Monitors and scans dependencies for vulnerabilities, ensuring libraries are secure and up-to-date.

Tools.Name: Dependabot
Tools.Description: Automates the creation of pull requests to update dependencies and fix vulnerabilities.

Tools.Name: Renovate
Tools.Description: Automates dependency updates and integrates seamlessly with CI/CD workflows.

2.Risk: Outdated dependencies increase the risk of exploitation by known vulnerabilities.
2.Measure: Evaluate dependencies with `libyear` to assess patch management efficiency.

2.Description: Security code reviews systematically identify and remediate vulnerabilities in source code, ensuring adherence to security best practices. Tools like SonarQube, GitHub CodeQL, and GitLab SAST automate code reviews within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Analyzes code for security vulnerabilities and quality issues.

Tools.Name: GitHub CodeQL
Tools.Description: Identifies vulnerabilities through semantic code analysis for GitHub-hosted projects.

Tools.Name: GitLab SAST
Tools.Description: Scans code for vulnerabilities within GitLab repositories.

Tools.Name: Checkmarx
Tools.Description: Scans code for security vulnerabilities using Static Application Security Testing (SAST).

Tools.Name: Fortify
Tools.Description: Identifies vulnerabilities in source code with comprehensive application security testing.

Tools.Name: ESLint
Tools.Description: Linting tool for JavaScript with customizable security-focused rules.

2.Risk: Security vulnerabilities may go unnoticed during development.
2.Measure: Prioritize reviewing high-risk code areas such as cryptographic implementations, authentication, and configuration management.

2.Description: Testing virtualized environments ensures that virtual machines and containers are securely configured and compliant with security standards. Tools like Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform automate the validation of virtualized environments within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Docker
Tools.Description: Develops, ships, and runs containerized applications, essential for virtual environment testing.

Tools.Name: Kubernetes
Tools.Description: Orchestrates and validates containerized environments for secure configurations.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Configures and manages virtual environments securely.

Tools.Name: Puppet
Tools.Description: Manages provisioning and hardening of virtualized environments.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Provisions and tests secure virtualized environments using Infrastructure as Code.

Tools.Name: VirtualBox
Tools.Description: Open-source tool for testing and validating virtual environments.

Tools.Name: VMware vSphere
Tools.Description: Manages virtual machines and ensures secure configurations in enterprise settings.

2.Risk: Virtualized environments may have insecure configurations, leading to potential vulnerabilities.
2.Measure: Regularly test virtualized environments to identify and fix insecure configurations.

2.Description: Application Hardening Level 1 implements basic security measures like input validation, error handling, and secure coding practices to protect applications. Tools like ESLint, SonarQube, and OWASP ZAP automate these measures in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: ESLint
Tools.Description: Enforces coding standards and identifies security vulnerabilities in JavaScript.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Analyzes code for security vulnerabilities and enforces quality standards.

Tools.Name: OWASP ZAP
Tools.Description: Scans web applications for vulnerabilities during CI/CD workflows.

2.Risk: Insecure applications may lead to data theft or unauthorized modifications.
2.Measure: Adopt OWASP Application Security Verification Standard Level 1 and Mobile Application Security Verification Standard. Implement 95%-100% of recommendations.
2.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

2.Description: Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enhances security by requiring multiple verification methods for access. Tools like Azure Active Directory, Okta, and Duo Security automate MFA enforcement within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Azure Active Directory
Tools.Description: Enforces MFA for secure authentication in cloud environments.

Tools.Name: Okta
Tools.Description: Provides identity management and MFA for secure user access.

Tools.Name: Duo Security
Tools.Description: Offers MFA solutions for secure authentication across platforms.

2.Risk: Single-factor authentication is vulnerable to brute-force attacks and compromises.
2.Measure: Enforce MFA for all accounts on critical systems and applications.

2.Description: Regular security training for all employees ensures familiarity with security policies, best practices, and emerging threats, fostering a security-conscious culture. Platforms like Moodle or Coursera can facilitate structured training programs.
2.Risk: Understanding security concepts is difficult without consistent training.
2.Measure: Conduct security training for all internal personnel involved in software development twice a year for 1-3 days.

2.Description: Pinning artifacts locks dependencies and libraries to specific, approved versions, ensuring that only secure and tested versions are used during the build and deployment processes. Tools like Dependabot and Renovate automate this process in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Dependabot
Tools.Description: Automates dependency updates and ensures secure versions in GitHub projects.

Tools.Name: Renovate
Tools.Description: Automates dependency updates and enforces artifact pinning.

Tools.Name: Snyk
Tools.Description: Scans and monitors dependencies for vulnerabilities.

Tools.Name: Artifactory
Tools.Description: Manages artifact repositories and enforces version pinning.

2.Risk: Unpinned artifacts may lead to the introduction of vulnerabilities or malicious code.
2.Measure: Lock dependencies and libraries to specific versions during builds.

2.Description: Generating a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) documents all components, dependencies, and versions used in the application. This inventory aids in identifying vulnerabilities and maintaining compliance. Tools like CycloneDX, SPDX, and Syft automate SBOM creation within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: CycloneDX
Tools.Description: Creates SBOMs for application security and supply chain analysis.

Tools.Name: SPDX
Tools.Description: Provides a standard for communicating SBOM information.

Tools.Name: Syft
Tools.Description: Generates SBOMs for container images and file systems.

Tools.Name: Anchore
Tools.Description: Performs deep container inspection and generates SBOMs.

2.Risk: Lack of visibility into software components can lead to vulnerabilities being overlooked.
2.Measure: Generate and maintain SBOMs for all components and dependencies.

2.Activity: Evaluation of the Trust of Used Components
2.Description: Evaluating the trustworthiness of components ensures that dependencies and system artifacts are secure and reliable. Tools like Black Duck, Snyk, and Sonatype Nexus Lifecycle automate this evaluation and enforce policies for trusted artifacts within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Black Duck
Tools.Description: Manages open-source risks by identifying and mitigating security vulnerabilities and license issues.

Tools.Name: Snyk
Tools.Description: Identifies and fixes vulnerabilities in dependencies and container images.

Tools.Name: Sonatype Nexus Lifecycle
Tools.Description: Provides governance and policy enforcement for managing open-source components.

2.Risk: Unverified components may contain vulnerabilities or malicious code, compromising the security of the production environment.
2.Measure: Evaluate each componentâ€™s source, maintainer practices, and trust level. Implement artifact whitelists for container images and virtual machine images.

2.Description: Regular automated tests ensure continuous code quality and security by identifying issues early. Tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, and GitHub Actions automate test executions within CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Jenkins
Tools.Description: Schedules and runs automated tests in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: GitLab CI/CD
Tools.Description: Executes regular automated test suites within deployment workflows.

Tools.Name: GitHub Actions
Tools.Description: Automates test executions using workflow configurations.

2.Risk: Delays in feedback on defects after code pushes can complicate remediation.
2.Measure: Run automated security tests on each code push or at scheduled intervals.

2.Description: Testing for exposed services identifies externally accessible endpoints to ensure they are secure. Tools like Nmap, Shodan, and OpenVAS automate this process in CI/CD pipelines to minimize attack surfaces.

Tools.Name: Nmap
Tools.Description: Scans networks for exposed services and vulnerabilities.

Tools.Name: Shodan
Tools.Description: Searches for internet-connected devices to identify exposed services.

Tools.Name: OpenVAS
Tools.Description: Vulnerability scanner for detecting exposed services.

2.Risk: Unsecured cluster management ports may be exposed due to improper segmentation.
2.Measure: Use tools to scan network configurations and identify unintended exposures.

2.Description: Establishing a clear decommissioning process ensures that unused applications are systematically removed from the 'Inventory of Production Components' and, where applicable, the 'Inventory of Production Artifacts.' This reduces risks associated with vulnerabilities in obsolete applications, minimizing their exploitation by attackers.

Tools.Name: ServiceNow
Tools.Description: A comprehensive IT service management platform that supports the automation and tracking of service and asset decommissioning processes.

Tools.Name: CMDB (e.g., BMC Helix, Device42)
Tools.Description: Configuration Management Database tools that facilitate inventory management and ensure streamlined decommissioning processes.

2.Risk: Unused applications may remain unmaintained and vulnerable, providing attackers with an entry point for lateral movement or exploitation.
2.Measure: Implement a systematic decommissioning process to remove unused applications from the inventory of production components and artifacts.

2.Description: Maintaining an up-to-date inventory of production artifacts, such as container images, ensures rapid identification and mitigation of vulnerabilities. This documentation can be gathered manually or automated to track where affected artifacts are deployed, improving response times to high-severity vulnerabilities.

Tools.Name: Anchore
Tools.Description: An open-source tool for inspecting and analyzing container images, aiding in maintaining an accurate inventory of production artifacts.

Tools.Name: Clair
Tools.Description: A vulnerability analysis tool for container images that helps organizations manage production artifacts with static vulnerability scans.

Tools.Name: AWS Artifact
Tools.Description: A service for accessing AWS compliance reports and managing production artifacts in cloud environments.

2.Risk: Without a documented inventory, it is challenging to locate and remediate vulnerable artifacts deployed in production environments.
2.Measure: Create and maintain an inventory of production artifacts, such as container images, either manually or through automated tools, to track deployments effectively.

1.Description: Providing security consulting ensures teams address specific challenges effectively. Communication tools like Slack and Teams facilitate consultations.
1.Risk: Failing to consult a security expert when needed may lead to security flaws.
1.Measure: Offer on-demand security consulting, either internally or externally.

1.Description: Versioning tracks changes in source code over time, enabling collaboration, rollback, and feature tracking. Proper versioning ensures documentation of all changes and efficient teamwork. Tools like Git, GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket automate version control within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Git
Tools.Description: Distributed version control system for tracking changes in source code during software development.

Tools.Name: GitHub
Tools.Description: Web-based platform using Git for version control, offering collaboration features like pull requests and issue tracking.

Tools.Name: GitLab
Tools.Description: Integrated DevOps platform offering Git repository management, CI/CD pipelines, and version control.

Tools.Name: Bitbucket
Tools.Description: Repository management solution with features like pull requests and branch permissions.

1.Risk: Deployment of untracked artifacts may lead to operational inefficiencies or security risks.
1.Measure: Ensure versioning of all artifacts, including application and infrastructure code, Jenkins configurations, and container or virtual machine images.

1.Description: Centralizing system logs enables efficient monitoring and troubleshooting of system-level events. Tools like Syslog, Graylog, and Splunk facilitate centralized log collection and analysis.

Tools.Name: Syslog
Tools.Description: Standard protocol for aggregating system logs into a centralized server.

Tools.Name: Graylog
Tools.Description: Open-source platform for managing and analyzing system logs.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Comprehensive tool for monitoring and analyzing machine-generated data.

1.Risk: Local logs can be manipulated or become inaccessible after incidents.
1.Measure: Centralize logs to prevent unauthorized modifications and ensure log availability.

1.Description: Managing false positives involves identifying and dismissing incorrect vulnerability alerts, reducing noise and focusing on genuine security issues. Tools like SonarQube, GitLab SAST, and ESLint automate the suppression of false positives in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Detects and marks false positives, reducing noise in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: GitLab SAST
Tools.Description: Static Application Security Testing tool for managing false positives within GitLab repositories.

Tools.Name: GitHub Advanced Security
Tools.Description: Allows marking specific security alerts as false positives within GitHub repositories.

Tools.Name: ESLint
Tools.Description: Static code analysis tool for JavaScript projects to manage and ignore specific findings.

Tools.Name: PyLint
Tools.Description: Static code analysis tool for Python, supporting false positive suppression.

Tools.Name: IDE Plugins (e.g., PyCharm, VSCode)
Tools.Description: Plugins for development environments to mark findings as false positives during the development process.

1.Risk: Frequent false positives may lead to ignoring genuine vulnerabilities.
1.Measure: Suppress false positives so they do not appear in subsequent tests. Use a Vulnerability Management System where possible.

1.Activity: Conduction of Simple Threat Modeling on Technical Level
1.Description: Performing basic threat modeling identifies potential security threats early in the design phase. This helps in proactively mitigating risks in system architecture. Methodologies like STRIDE and tools such as Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool can standardize the process.
1.Risk: Technical threats may remain undetected until later stages of development and deployment.
1.Measure: Incorporate threat modeling during sprint planning to proactively address technical risks.
1.ISO 27001:2022: May be part of risk assessment

1.Description: Partial implementation of Application Hardening Level 1 focuses on some security measures, like input validation and error handling, but lacks comprehensive coverage. Tools like ESLint and SonarQube assist in enforcing these partial measures.

Tools.Name: ESLint
Tools.Description: Linting tool for JavaScript that enforces partial coding standards.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Analyzes code for partial implementation of security and quality standards.

1.Risk: Insecure applications may lead to data breaches or unauthorized modifications.
1.Measure: Adopt OWASP Application Security Verification Standard Level 1 and Mobile Application Security Verification Standard. Implement 50% of recommendations.
1.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

1.Description: Securing administrator accounts with Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds additional verification layers to prevent unauthorized access and potential system compromises. Tools like Azure Active Directory, Okta, and Duo Security enforce MFA for admin accounts.

Tools.Name: Azure Active Directory
Tools.Description: Enforces MFA for administrative accounts in cloud environments.

Tools.Name: Okta
Tools.Description: Provides MFA for securing admin access across applications.

Tools.Name: Duo Security
Tools.Description: Integrates MFA with various platforms to secure admin authentication.

1.Risk: Single-factor authentication leaves administrative accounts vulnerable to brute force attacks.
1.Measure: Implement two or more factors for all privileged accounts on systems and applications.

1.Activity: Ad-Hoc Security Trainings for Software Developers
1.Description: Occasional security training sessions enhance developers' understanding of security best practices and awareness. Platforms like Pluralsight and Udemy provide flexible training modules that can be integrated into onboarding processes.
1.Risk: Without training, developers may unintentionally introduce security flaws like SQL Injection vulnerabilities.
1.Measure: Provide ad-hoc security awareness training for all software development personnel.

1.Description: Ensures secure handling of data based on its context to prevent injection attacks. Tools like ESLint and SonarQube enforce encoding standards in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: ESLint
Tools.Description: Linting tool for JavaScript and TypeScript, enforcing contextual encoding.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Analyzes code for proper contextual encoding to prevent injection vulnerabilities.

1.Risk: Improper data encoding can lead to injection vulnerabilities.
1.Measure: Use contextualized encoding, prepared statements, or ORM tools to eliminate injection risks.
1.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

1.Activity: Definition of Simple BCDR Practices for Critical Components
1.Description: Simple Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery (BCDR) practices ensure application and system availability during emergencies. These practices outline recovery point objectives (RPOs), recovery time objectives (RTOs), SLAs, and failover strategies.

Tools.Name: Azure Site Recovery
Tools.Description: Ensures business continuity by keeping workloads running during outages.

Tools.Name: Veeam Backup & Replication
Tools.Description: Provides backup and recovery solutions to ensure data availability.

Tools.Name: Disaster Recovery Plan Templates
Tools.Description: Templates for documenting effective BCDR strategies and procedures.

1.Risk: Lack of documented recovery practices may result in extended downtime during emergencies.
1.Measure: Document clear recovery procedures and responsibilities for critical components.

1.Description: Maintaining an inventory of production components ensures comprehensive tracking and management of all software and hardware elements in the production environment. This enhances visibility, facilitates efficient maintenance, and supports compliance and auditing processes. Tools like Ansible, Puppet, and Terraform, integrated into CI/CD pipelines, automate inventory updates.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Automates inventory management using playbooks to track production components within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Puppet
Tools.Description: Provides configuration management capabilities to automate inventory updates and component tracking.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Infrastructure as Code tool that supports provisioning and inventory tracking of production components.

Tools.Name: AWS Config
Tools.Description: Automates compliance checks and inventory tracking of AWS resources in production environments.

1.Risk: Lack of awareness of deployed production components increases risks of unmanaged and vulnerable systems.
1.Measure: Maintain a documented inventory of production components, updated manually or automatically using tools.

1.Description: Fixing defects based on severity ensures that high-impact issues are addressed promptly while less critical issues are scheduled for future remediation. This approach helps teams manage risks effectively and allocate resources optimally. Tools like Jira, GitLab, and SonarQube automate prioritization and tracking of defect resolution within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Jira
Tools.Description: Manages and tracks defect remediation by categorizing them based on severity levels.

Tools.Name: GitLab
Tools.Description: Facilitates defect prioritization and resolution through integrated CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Categorizes defects by severity and automates tracking and remediation within CI/CD pipelines.

1.Risk: Overwhelming findings from automated tools might lead to ignoring critical vulnerabilities, increasing the risk of exploitation.
1.Measure: Apply a simple risk-based prioritization framework for remediation, focusing on critical findings at the initial level.

5.Description: Coverage analysis in dynamic testing measures how much of an application's code is exercised during tests. It identifies untested parts of the codebase, ensuring comprehensive testing and minimizing undiscovered vulnerabilities. Tools like JaCoCo, Istanbul, Coverage.py, and Codecov assist in measuring and visualizing code coverage.

Tools.Name: JaCoCo
Tools.Description: Java code coverage library to analyze test coverage.

Tools.Name: Istanbul
Tools.Description: JavaScript tool for analyzing and visualizing code coverage.

Tools.Name: Coverage.py
Tools.Description: Python tool to measure and report code coverage.

Tools.Name: Clover
Tools.Description: Tool for measuring test coverage for Java and Groovy applications.

Tools.Name: Bullseye
Tools.Description: Tool for measuring code coverage for C and C++ applications.

Tools.Name: DotCover
Tools.Description: Analyzes code coverage for .NET applications.

5.Risk: Untested parts of the application may harbor undetected vulnerabilities.
5.Measure: Ensure complete application path coverage using coverage tools.
5.ISO 27001:2022: Coverage is not explicitly mapped in ISO 27001

5.Description: Combining performance and security metrics with testing provides a comprehensive evaluation of application behavior and security posture. Tools like Grafana, Prometheus, and Splunk enable visualization and analysis of integrated metrics and test results.

Tools.Name: Grafana
Tools.Description: Open-source platform for monitoring and visualizing metrics.

Tools.Name: Prometheus
Tools.Description: Monitoring system and time-series database for test integration.

Tools.Name: Datadog
Tools.Description: Monitoring and analytics platform that integrates metrics with testing tools.

Tools.Name: New Relic
Tools.Description: Application performance monitoring tool combining metrics and test data.

Tools.Name: ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana)
Tools.Description: Comprehensive solution for monitoring and test metric integration.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Platform for analyzing machine-generated data, including test metrics.

5.Risk: Changes may cause high loads or programming errors during testing.
5.Measure: Analyze metrics during tests to identify and address errors early.

5.Description: Analyzing and linking security events from multiple sources helps detect patterns, identify threats, and respond effectively to incidents. Tools like Splunk, IBM QRadar, and ArcSight automate correlation and analysis of security events.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Platform for correlating and analyzing security events.

Tools.Name: IBM QRadar
Tools.Description: SIEM solution for detecting threats through event correlation.

Tools.Name: ArcSight
Tools.Description: Provides SIEM capabilities for analyzing and correlating security events.

5.Risk: Unlinked security events make detecting complex attacks difficult.
5.Measure: Correlate events in a centralized system to identify and respond to threats.

5.Description: Establishing guidelines for securely logging Personally Identifiable Information (PII) ensures compliance with data protection regulations and prevents unauthorized access. This includes data masking, encryption, and controlled access to logs containing PII. Tools like Splunk, Logstash, and Fluentd enforce PII protection measures in DevSecOps pipelines.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Provides secure logging with support for data masking and encryption to ensure PII protection.

Tools.Name: Logstash
Tools.Description: Processes data pipelines to mask and encrypt PII, integrated into CI/CD workflows for secure logging.

Tools.Name: Fluentd
Tools.Description: Open-source data collector that applies PII protection through data transformation and encryption.

5.Risk: Failure to comply with privacy laws like GDPR due to unprotected PII in logs.
5.Measure: Document and enforce a concept for securely logging PII.

5.Description: Identifying and removing unused or redundant code improves maintainability, reduces vulnerabilities, and optimizes performance. Tools like SonarQube, ESLint, and PMD support automated dead code detection and elimination in CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Detects dead code and other code quality issues, integrating seamlessly with CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: ESLint
Tools.Description: Analyzes JavaScript projects to identify and eliminate dead code in CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: PMD
Tools.Description: Analyzes source code to detect dead code and other potential issues in Java and other languages.

5.Risk: Dead code increases the attack surface, such as through hardcoded credentials or sensitive information.
5.Measure: Identify unused code and manually remove it to ensure clean and optimized codebases.

5.Description: Removing duplicate code segments enhances maintainability, reduces vulnerabilities, and improves code quality. Tools like SonarQube, PMD, and CodeClimate automate detection and elimination of duplicate code within CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Detects duplicate code and quality issues, seamlessly integrating with CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: PMD
Tools.Description: Analyzes source code to identify duplicates and other issues across multiple programming languages.

Tools.Name: CodeClimate
Tools.Description: Provides duplicate code detection and quality metrics with CI/CD pipeline integration.

5.Risk: Duplicate code can increase complexity and reduce application stability.
5.Measure: Detect duplicates automatically and refactor code to remove redundancy.

5.Description: Advanced abuse stories simulate detailed and complex attack scenarios to identify vulnerabilities, enhance security, and improve incident response strategies. Frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK provide structured approaches for developing these stories.
5.Risk: Basic user stories fail to address deeper security considerations, delaying the discovery of critical flaws.
5.Measure: Develop advanced abuse stories as part of threat modeling to explore sophisticated attack scenarios.
5.ISO 27001:2022: May be part of project management and risk assessment

5.Description: Adopting a microservice architecture structures applications as collections of loosely coupled, independently deployable services. This approach enhances scalability, flexibility, and resilience while introducing new security considerations. Tools like Kubernetes, Docker, and Istio automate the deployment and management of microservices in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Kubernetes
Tools.Description: Manages deployment, scaling, and operation of application containers in a microservice architecture.

Tools.Name: Docker
Tools.Description: Platform for developing and running containerized applications, essential for microservices.

Tools.Name: Istio
Tools.Description: Service mesh providing secure, connected, and observable communication between microservices.

Tools.Name: Linkerd
Tools.Description: Lightweight service mesh for Kubernetes, optimizing performance and simplicity.

Tools.Name: Consul
Tools.Description: Provides service discovery, configuration, and segmentation in a microservice environment.

Tools.Name: Envoy
Tools.Description: High-performance proxy for service meshes in cloud-native applications.

Tools.Name: Helm
Tools.Description: Simplifies deployment and management of microservices in Kubernetes through package management.

5.Risk: Monolithic applications are challenging to test and maintain.
5.Measure: Adopt microservice architecture to simplify testing and improve modularity.

5.Activity: Conduction of Collaborative Security Checks with Developers and System Administrators
5.Description: Joint security checks between developers and system administrators ensure alignment on security best practices, fostering continuous security improvement. Tools like GitLab CI/CD and Jenkins integrate security checks into collaborative workflows.

Tools.Name: GitLab CI/CD
Tools.Description: Integrates security checks into collaborative development workflows.

Tools.Name: Jenkins
Tools.Description: Orchestrates collaborative security check pipelines involving multiple teams.

5.Risk: External security checks may not increase internal understanding of applications and systems.
5.Measure: Conduct periodic security reviews with participation from developers, operations, and security SMEs to improve robustness and knowledge.
5.ISO 27001:2022: Mutual review of source code is not explicitly required in ISO 27001

5.Description: Deploying advanced Web Application Firewalls (WAF) with features like real-time threat detection, automated rule updates, and CI/CD integration ensures robust application security. Tools like AWS WAF, Cloudflare WAF, and Imperva automate WAF protection.

Tools.Name: AWS WAF
Tools.Description: Provides scalable WAF services with seamless CI/CD integration.

Tools.Name: Cloudflare WAF
Tools.Description: Offers automated rule updates and advanced threat protection.

Tools.Name: Imperva
Tools.Description: Delivers advanced WAF solutions with real-time threat intelligence.

5.Risk: Advanced threats such as zero-day vulnerabilities require rigorous input validation and defense strategies.
5.Measure: Implement input validation, reject unrequired parameters, and deploy dynamically updated rule sets.
5.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

5.Activity: Local Development Linting & Style Checks Performed
5.Description: Static code analysis tools integrated into IDEs ensure adherence to coding standards and secure practices. This reduces vulnerabilities and improves maintainability by identifying issues early in the development process.

Tools.Name: ESLint
Tools.Description: Linting tool for JavaScript and TypeScript to identify and fix issues.

Tools.Name: Pylint
Tools.Description: Static code analyzer for Python to enforce coding standards and detect issues.

Tools.Name: Rubocop
Tools.Description: Static code analyzer and formatter for Ruby, enforcing the Ruby style guide.

Tools.Name: StyleCop
Tools.Description: Analyzes C# code to enforce consistency and quality.

Tools.Name: Checkstyle
Tools.Description: Helps developers write Java code adhering to coding standards.

Tools.Name: SonarLint
Tools.Description: IDE extension providing real-time feedback on code quality and security.

5.Description: Comprehensive implementation of advanced security measures, including security integration throughout the development lifecycle and continuous monitoring. Tools like OWASP ZAP, Fortify, Snyk, and SonarQube automate these practices within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: OWASP ZAP
Tools.Description: Scans for comprehensive vulnerabilities during CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Fortify
Tools.Description: Performs extensive static and dynamic analysis for full-scale security hardening.

Tools.Name: Snyk
Tools.Description: Detects vulnerabilities in dependencies and container images.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Provides in-depth code quality and security analysis.

5.Risk: Lack of comprehensive security measures could lead to system compromise.
5.Measure: Adopt OWASP ASVS Level 3 and implement 95%-100% of the recommendations.
5.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

5.Description: Blue/Green Deployment reduces deployment risk by running two identical environments (Blue and Green) and switching between them during deployments. Tools like Kubernetes, AWS Elastic Beanstalk, and Spinnaker automate routing and switching processes for seamless deployments.

Tools.Name: Kubernetes
Tools.Description: Orchestrates containerized applications, enabling automated Blue/Green deployments.

Tools.Name: AWS Elastic Beanstalk
Tools.Description: Simplifies deployment and scaling of web applications across environments.

Tools.Name: Spinnaker
Tools.Description: Facilitates multi-cloud continuous delivery with Blue/Green deployment capabilities.

5.Risk: New artifact versions may contain unknown defects.
5.Measure: Switching between Blue and Green environments ensures availability and simplifies rollbacks in case of deployment failures.

5.Description: Identifying and removing unused infrastructure components reduces security risks and optimizes resource usage. Tools like AWS Trusted Advisor, Azure Resource Manager, and Google Cloud Resource Manager automate these checks in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: AWS Trusted Advisor
Tools.Description: Optimizes AWS resources by identifying unused components.

Tools.Name: Azure Resource Manager
Tools.Description: Manages Azure resources, identifying and removing unused elements.

Tools.Name: Google Cloud Resource Manager
Tools.Description: Manages and optimizes Google Cloud resources, identifying unused components.

5.Risk: Unused resources, such as active secrets, may expose valid credentials.
5.Measure: Perform regular scans to detect and manage unused resources.

4.Description: Tracking coverage and control metrics ensures the effectiveness of security controls and identifies areas for improvement. Tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and Splunk provide comprehensive monitoring and visualization capabilities to support continuous compliance with security standards.

Tools.Name: Prometheus
Tools.Description: Open-source monitoring and alerting toolkit.

Tools.Name: Grafana
Tools.Description: Open-source observability platform for monitoring and data visualization.

Tools.Name: Datadog
Tools.Description: Monitoring and analytics platform for IT and development operations.

Tools.Name: New Relic
Tools.Description: Performance monitoring and analytics tool for applications.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Platform for searching, monitoring, and analyzing machine-generated data.

Tools.Name: Nagios
Tools.Description: Open-source monitoring system for networks, applications, and infrastructure.

Tools.Name: Zabbix
Tools.Description: Enterprise-class open-source monitoring solution for performance and availability.

4.Risk: Ineffective monitoring may lead to undetected security gaps and non-compliance.
4.Measure: Use monitoring tools to assess and visualize security coverage metrics effectively.

4.Description: Conducting war games simulates real-world cyberattack scenarios to test incident response capabilities and identify security weaknesses. This enhances preparedness and strengthens overall security posture. Tools like SimSpace and BattleEye can assist in automating and integrating war games into CI/CD pipelines.
4.Risk: Ineffective understanding of incident response plans during a real incident.
4.Measure: War games allow trainees to practice responding to incidents in a controlled environment using scenarios created by security SMEs.

4.Description: Using a .gitignore file prevents sensitive or unnecessary files from being tracked in version control, enhancing security and streamlining CI/CD processes.
4.Risk: Sensitive data or unnecessary files may be unintentionally committed to version control.
4.Measure: Implement .gitignore files to exclude secrets, debug files, or other workstation-specific data.

4.Description: Using a Chaos Monkey introduces intentional failures to test system resilience and fault tolerance. This helps identify weaknesses and ensures infrastructure robustness. Tools like Gremlin, Chaos Toolkit, and AWS Fault Injection Simulator automate chaos experiments.

Tools.Name: Gremlin
Tools.Description: Simulates system failures to test resilience and fault tolerance.

Tools.Name: Chaos Toolkit
Tools.Description: Open-source tool for running chaos experiments to improve system reliability.

Tools.Name: AWS Fault Injection Simulator
Tools.Description: Runs chaos engineering experiments on AWS infrastructure.

4.Risk: Manual system changes may result in irreproducible setups and increased risk during failures.
4.Measure: Periodically shut down systems randomly to prevent reliance on manual configurations.

4.Description: Designing dashboards for visualizing performance and security metrics provides real-time insights, enabling better decision-making and faster incident response. Tools like Grafana, Kibana, and Datadog automate the creation of metric dashboards in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Grafana
Tools.Description: Creates interactive dashboards for visualizing performance and security metrics.

Tools.Name: Kibana
Tools.Description: Visualization tool for the ELK Stack, providing customizable metric dashboards.

Tools.Name: Datadog
Tools.Description: Analytics platform with built-in capabilities for creating metric dashboards.

4.Risk: Security incidents may go unnoticed without proper visualization of metrics.
4.Measure: Implement accessible dashboards to visualize and monitor security metrics in real-time.

4.Activity: Conduction of Collaborative Team Security Checks
4.Description: Collaborative team security checks involve teams working together to review the security of applications and infrastructure. This approach enhances shared responsibility, knowledge sharing, and comprehensive risk identification. Tools like Jira and Confluence facilitate collaboration and tracking of security checks.

Tools.Name: Jira
Tools.Description: Tracks and manages collaborative security check tasks within project workflows.

Tools.Name: Confluence
Tools.Description: Documents and shares findings and strategies from security checks.

4.Risk: Development teams have limited insight into security practices.
4.Measure: Encourage mutual security testing of projects between teams to enhance awareness and knowledge.
4.ISO 27001:2022: Mutual security testing is not explicitly required by ISO 27001

4.Description: Applying comprehensive security measures, including secure configurations, removal of unnecessary services, and robust access controls, to harden environments against vulnerabilities. Tools like Ansible, Puppet, and Chef automate the hardening process in CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Enforces comprehensive security configurations and hardening measures across environments.

Tools.Name: Puppet
Tools.Description: Automates the application of security hardening measures to infrastructure components.

Tools.Name: Chef
Tools.Description: Manages and enforces secure configurations and hardening processes.

4.Risk: Default configurations in cluster environments may introduce vulnerabilities.
4.Measure: Follow best practices, such as Level 2 and partially Level 3 of the CIS Kubernetes Bench for Security.
4.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

4.Description: Integrating security objectives into team workflows ensures shared responsibility for security. Tools like Jira and Confluence support the alignment of security tasks with team operations.

Tools.Name: Jira
Tools.Description: Tracks security tasks and integrates them into team workflows.

Tools.Name: Confluence
Tools.Description: Facilitates documentation and sharing of security guidelines across teams.

4.Risk: Relying on a single security champion may lead to the misconception that only one person is responsible for security.
4.Measure: Align security Subject Matter Experts with project teams to raise the overall security standard.

4.Description: Advanced threat modeling identifies security risks by analyzing user stories and creating security-focused data flow diagrams. This process proactively mitigates business and technical risks, enhancing application security.

Tools.Name: Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool
Tools.Description: Supports creating data flow diagrams and identifying threats using predefined methodologies.

Tools.Name: ThreatModeler
Tools.Description: Automates threat modeling and integrates with development workflows to identify risks.

Tools.Name: OWASP Threat Dragon
Tools.Description: An open-source tool for collaborative creation and analysis of threat models.

4.Description: Application Hardening Level 2 implements advanced measures like security headers, secure authentication mechanisms, and session management to protect against advanced threats. Tools like OWASP ZAP, Fortify, and Snyk automate these measures in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: OWASP ZAP
Tools.Description: Scans web applications for advanced vulnerabilities.

Tools.Name: Fortify
Tools.Description: Performs static and dynamic analysis to identify advanced security vulnerabilities.

Tools.Name: Snyk
Tools.Description: Detects vulnerabilities in dependencies and container images.

4.Risk: Insecure applications may result in data theft or system compromise.
4.Measure: Follow OWASP ASVS Level 2 and implement 95%-100% of the recommendations.
4.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

4.Description: Medium-level WAF implementation enhances security rules and adds monitoring capabilities. Tools like AWS WAF, Cloudflare WAF, and Imperva offer advanced features like real-time analysis and custom rules.

Tools.Name: AWS WAF
Tools.Description: Provides medium-level configurations with real-time traffic monitoring.

Tools.Name: Cloudflare WAF
Tools.Description: Offers customizable rules and CI/CD integration for continuous protection.

Tools.Name: Imperva
Tools.Description: Delivers advanced threat detection and ongoing security management.

4.Risk: Malicious inputs remain a threat to application security.
4.Measure: Enhance WAF configurations to detect threats effectively while minimizing false alarms.
4.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

4.Description: Using the same artifact for all environments ensures consistency and reduces the risk of untested changes being deployed to production.

Tools.Name: Docker
Tools.Description: Containerizes applications, ensuring consistent artifacts across environments.

Tools.Name: Kubernetes
Tools.Description: Manages containerized applications for consistent deployment across environments.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Automates infrastructure as code to standardize environment deployments.

4.Risk: Different artifacts for each environment may introduce untested changes to production.
4.Measure: Build artifacts once and deploy them across all environments.

4.Description: Feature toggles enable or disable features dynamically without requiring code changes or redeployments. Using static feature toggles, independent of environment variables, mitigates the risk of inadvertently enabling insecure features in production. Tools like LaunchDarkly, Unleash, and FeatureToggle automate the management and deployment of feature flags within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: LaunchDarkly
Tools.Description: Manages feature releases using feature flags for precise control.

Tools.Name: Unleash
Tools.Description: Open-source feature toggle system for managing feature releases.

Tools.Name: FeatureToggle
Tools.Description: Manages feature flags to control feature visibility within applications.

4.Risk: Using environment variables to toggle features may unintentionally enable insecure features in production.
4.Measure: Adopt static feature toggles to ensure environment-independent configurations.

4.Description: Load tests simulate high-traffic scenarios to evaluate application and infrastructure performance under stress. Tools like JMeter, Locust, and Gatling automate load testing within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: JMeter
Tools.Description: Open-source tool for load testing and performance measurement.

Tools.Name: Locust
Tools.Description: Python-based tool for scalable load testing.

Tools.Name: Gatling
Tools.Description: High-performance load testing tool using Scala.

4.Risk: Unknown load capacity can cause availability issues under unexpected traffic.
4.Measure: Perform load tests on production or near-production environments.

4.Description: Advanced visualization of defects enables teams to monitor software quality and security comprehensively by creating interactive dashboards. These dashboards display defect metrics, trends, and patterns, offering actionable insights for proactive improvement. Tools like SonarQube, Jira, and Grafana streamline data aggregation and visualization within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Aggregates defect data and offers advanced visualizations via dashboards to enhance software quality insights.

Tools.Name: Jira
Tools.Description: Tracks defect metrics and integrates with tools like SonarQube to provide actionable insights into defect trends.

Tools.Name: Grafana
Tools.Description: Creates detailed and interactive dashboards to visualize defect data from various sources in real-time.

4.Risk: Lack of correlation among vulnerabilities detected by different tools reduces the overall understanding of security posture per component/project/team.
4.Measure: Visualize findings across components, projects, and teams to provide comprehensive defect and vulnerability insights.

3.Description: Testing and verifying hidden endpoints ensures all access points in the application's API are secured and free from vulnerabilities. Tools like Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP, and Postman assist in identifying and testing hidden endpoints.

Tools.Name: Burp Suite
Tools.Description: Integrated platform for web application security testing.

Tools.Name: OWASP ZAP
Tools.Description: Open-source tool for automated security scanning of web applications.

Tools.Name: Postman
Tools.Description: API testing and development platform.

Tools.Name: SoapUI
Tools.Description: Tool for functional testing of SOAP and REST web services.

Tools.Name: Fiddler
Tools.Description: Web debugging proxy for HTTP/HTTPS traffic monitoring and modification.

Tools.Name: Insomnia
Tools.Description: Open-source API client for testing REST and GraphQL.

3.Risk: Hidden endpoints may go untested, leaving potential vulnerabilities unaddressed.
3.Measure: Ensure hidden endpoints are identified and included in vulnerability scans.

3.Description: Testing applications with a wide range of input vectors identifies potential vulnerabilities and ensures robustness against unexpected or malicious inputs. Tools like FuzzDB, Burp Suite Intruder, and Radamsa assist in comprehensive input testing.

Tools.Name: FuzzDB
Tools.Description: Database of attack patterns and predictable resource locations for security testing.

Tools.Name: Burp Suite Intruder
Tools.Description: Automated tool for fuzzing and injection attack testing.

Tools.Name: OWASP ZAP Fuzzer
Tools.Description: Fuzzing tool integrated into OWASP ZAP for dynamic testing.

Tools.Name: Radamsa
Tools.Description: Fuzzing and robustness testing tool for generating test cases.

Tools.Name: Wfuzz
Tools.Description: Fuzzing and brute-forcing tool for web applications.

Tools.Name: AFL (American Fuzzy Lop)
Tools.Description: Security-oriented fuzzer for identifying software vulnerabilities.

3.Risk: Input vectors not covered during testing may allow injection or unexpected behavior vulnerabilities.
3.Measure: Expand input vector testing to cover all possible application inputs.

3.Description: Filtering outgoing traffic restricts and monitors data leaving the organization's network, preventing unauthorized data exfiltration and malware communication. Tools like AWS Network Firewall, Azure Firewall, and Cisco ASA automate outbound traffic filtering.

Tools.Name: AWS Network Firewall
Tools.Description: Managed service for filtering outgoing traffic in AWS environments.

Tools.Name: Azure Firewall
Tools.Description: Cloud-based service for protecting Azure Virtual Network resources.

Tools.Name: Cisco ASA
Tools.Description: Firewall appliance for monitoring and filtering outgoing traffic.

3.Risk: A compromised infrastructure component may attempt to exfiltrate stolen data.
3.Measure: Implement a whitelist to restrict outgoing traffic to authorized destinations.

3.Description: Restricting the types and frequency of system events focuses log management on critical security-related information, reducing noise and improving efficiency. Tools like Syslog-ng, Logstash, and Splunk enable effective management of system events.

Tools.Name: Syslog-ng
Tools.Description: Advanced syslog protocol with filtering and processing capabilities.

Tools.Name: Logstash
Tools.Description: Pipeline for ingesting, transforming, and forwarding logs.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Platform for analyzing machine-generated data with advanced log filtering.

Tools.Name: Graylog
Tools.Description: Open-source log management tool with filtering capabilities.

Tools.Name: ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana)
Tools.Description: Comprehensive logging and visualization platform.

Tools.Name: Fluentd
Tools.Description: Unified logging tool for collecting and processing log data.

Tools.Name: Nagios
Tools.Description: Monitoring tool capable of limiting and filtering system events.

3.Risk: Unrestricted system events may result in privilege escalation or system overload.
3.Measure: Restrict and monitor system calls to essential operations.

3.Activity: Approval by Reviewing Any New Version
3.Description: Mandating security and quality checks before deploying new software versions ensures releases meet organizational security standards, reducing vulnerabilities. Tools like GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, and Jenkins automate review and approval processes in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: GitHub Actions
Tools.Description: Automates the approval and review process for new software versions.

Tools.Name: GitLab CI/CD
Tools.Description: Enforces approval workflows and security checks before deploying new versions.

Tools.Name: Jenkins
Tools.Description: Orchestrates approval and review processes for software releases.

3.Risk: Security measures may be omitted, exposing source code or infrastructure components to risks.
3.Measure: Implement a peer review process (two eyes principle) where changes are reviewed and approved by another individual before deployment.
3.ISO 27001:2022: Peer review (four eyes principle) is not explicitly required by ISO 27001

3.Description: Automatically revoking approvals for Pull Requests (PRs) that are invalidated by subsequent changes ensures code quality and security standards are maintained. Tools like GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, and Jenkins automate this process in CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: GitHub Actions
Tools.Description: Automates the dismissal of stale PR approvals based on predefined criteria.

Tools.Name: GitLab CI/CD
Tools.Description: Manages and automates the dismissal of stale PR approvals.

Tools.Name: Jenkins
Tools.Description: Orchestrates dismissal of stale PR approvals within CI/CD pipelines.

3.Risk: Code modifications after approval may introduce vulnerabilities in critical branches.
3.Measure: Implement a policy where any changes made after PR approval automatically revoke the approval, requiring re-review.
3.ISO 27001:2022: Peer review (four eyes principle) is not explicitly required by ISO 27001

3.Description: Immutable infrastructure ensures components are never modified after deployment; any updates result in redeployment. This approach reduces the attack surface and ensures consistency. Tools like Terraform, Kubernetes, and Docker automate immutable infrastructure deployment within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Infrastructure as Code tool for managing and automating immutable infrastructure deployments.

Tools.Name: Kubernetes
Tools.Description: Manages immutable deployments by deploying and scaling containerized applications without modifying existing instances.

Tools.Name: Docker
Tools.Description: Platform for creating and deploying containerized applications supporting immutable infrastructure.

3.Risk: System availability may be disrupted due to component failures.
3.Measure: Introduce redundancies in IT systems to mitigate failures.

3.Description: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) uses machine-readable configuration files to manage and provision computing infrastructure. This ensures consistency, repeatability, and version control, enhancing security and efficiency. Tools like Terraform, Ansible, and Pulumi support IaC implementations.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Open-source tool for safely and efficiently building, changing, and versioning infrastructure.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Automation tool for configuring and managing infrastructure as code.

Tools.Name: Pulumi
Tools.Description: Allows defining cloud resources using programming languages.

3.Risk: Lack of change tracking in systems may lead to configuration errors or unauthorized changes.
3.Measure: Systems are managed via code and stored in a version control system to ensure traceability.

3.Description: Aggregating logs from applications into a centralized system simplifies monitoring, troubleshooting, and security event analysis. Tools like ELK Stack, Splunk, and Fluentd automate log collection and centralization.

Tools.Name: ELK Stack
Tools.Description: Suite (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) for centralized logging, visualization, and analysis.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Platform for analyzing and monitoring machine-generated data.

Tools.Name: Fluentd
Tools.Description: Open-source tool for unified logging and centralized log management.

3.Risk: Local logs may be manipulated or corrupted, making it hard to detect attacks.
3.Measure: Centralize application logs and include exception logs in the system.

3.Description: Identifying and deactivating unused metrics reduces resource consumption and focuses monitoring on critical metrics. Tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog can automate this process within DevSecOps pipelines.

Tools.Name: Prometheus
Tools.Description: Open-source monitoring toolkit for identifying and deactivating unused metrics in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Grafana
Tools.Description: Visualization tool that manages and deactivates unused metrics for optimized monitoring.

Tools.Name: Datadog
Tools.Description: Monitoring platform that optimizes resource usage by deactivating unused metrics.

3.Risk: Unused metrics consume excessive resources.
3.Measure: Deactivate unused metrics to optimize resource usage.

3.Description: Organizing related metrics into groups enhances clarity and efficiency during monitoring and analysis. Tools like Grafana, Prometheus, and Datadog automate grouping and visualization of metrics in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Grafana
Tools.Description: Organizes metrics into dashboards for streamlined monitoring and analysis.

Tools.Name: Prometheus
Tools.Description: Supports grouping and monitoring of related metrics within CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Datadog
Tools.Description: Enables grouping of related metrics for better analysis and organization.

3.Risk: Delayed analysis of unorganized metrics may slow response times.
3.Measure: Group metrics meaningfully to speed up analysis.

3.Description: Targeted alerting focuses on creating specific alerts for critical events or thresholds, ensuring timely responses to high-impact incidents. Tools like PagerDuty, Opsgenie, and Datadog automate targeted alerting in CI/CD pipelines to enhance operational efficiency.

Tools.Name: PagerDuty
Tools.Description: Manages and routes alerts to ensure prompt resolution of incidents.

Tools.Name: Opsgenie
Tools.Description: Ensures critical alerts are routed to the appropriate responders for immediate action.

Tools.Name: Datadog
Tools.Description: Customizable monitoring platform for creating alerts based on metrics and events.

3.Risk: Non-relevant incident alarms may cause alert fatigue, reducing responsiveness.
3.Measure: Define target groups for incidents to ensure only responsible individuals receive relevant alerts.

3.Activity: Integration of Vulnerability Issues into the Development Process
3.Description: Embedding vulnerability tracking and remediation into the development process ensures that security issues are addressed early in the SDLC. Tools like SonarQube, GitLab CI/CD, and Snyk automate the identification and resolution of vulnerabilities within CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: SonarQube
Tools.Description: Analyzes code for vulnerabilities and integrates with CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: GitLab CI/CD
Tools.Description: Manages continuous integration and delivery while tracking vulnerabilities within GitLab repositories.

Tools.Name: Snyk
Tools.Description: Developer-first tool that identifies and remediates vulnerabilities in dependencies and container images.

3.Risk: Developers may overlook console output or fail to check vulnerability management systems regularly.
3.Measure: Track vulnerabilities within the team's issue tracking system (e.g., Jira) to ensure visibility and resolution.

3.Description: Measuring and optimizing patch deployment time ensures that security patches are applied promptly, minimizing vulnerability exposure. Tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, and Ansible automate patch deployment tracking and optimization in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Jenkins
Tools.Description: Automates patch deployment processes and tracks deployment times.

Tools.Name: GitLab CI/CD
Tools.Description: Facilitates automated patch deployments and measures deployment times.

Tools.Name: Ansible
Tools.Description: Automates patch deployments and monitors efficiency, integrating with CI/CD pipelines.

3.Risk: Delays in applying patches leave systems vulnerable to exploitation.
3.Measure: Continuously monitor and improve patch deployment times.

3.Description: Developing simple abuse stories outlines scenarios where attackers exploit common vulnerabilities. These stories help identify basic security gaps and guide the creation of effective controls. Tools like OWASP Threat Dragon can assist in visualizing these scenarios within workflows.
3.Risk: User stories may overlook security implications, leading to late discovery of security flaws during development and deployment.
3.Measure: Incorporate abuse stories into the creation of user stories to address potential vulnerabilities early.

3.Activity: Creation of Threat Modeling Processes and Standards
3.Description: Establishing structured methodologies and guidelines for threat modeling ensures consistent and thorough identification, assessment, and mitigation of security threats. Tools like Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool help standardize these processes across the organization.

Tools.Name: Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool
Tools.Description: Enables the creation and management of threat models to identify and address security risks.

3.Risk: Lack of standardized processes may result in inconsistent threat identification and mitigation.
3.Measure: Develop and implement threat modeling processes and standards to improve the organizationâ€™s security posture.
3.ISO 27001:2022: May be part of risk assessment

3.Description: Log analysis involves examining system and application logs to detect patterns, anomalies, and potential security incidents. Tools like ELK Stack, Splunk, and Graylog automate log collection and analysis within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana)
Tools.Description: Aggregates, visualizes, and analyzes logs for enhanced monitoring and security.

Tools.Name: Splunk
Tools.Description: Monitors and analyzes machine-generated data, including logs, for real-time insights.

Tools.Name: Graylog
Tools.Description: Open-source platform for centralized log management and analysis.

Tools.Name: Fluentd
Tools.Description: Collects and processes logs for unified log management.

Tools.Name: Loggly
Tools.Description: Cloud-based platform for real-time log analysis and management.

Tools.Name: Papertrail
Tools.Description: Cloud-hosted service for centralized log management and analysis.

Tools.Name: Datadog Logs
Tools.Description: Integrated logging service for comprehensive analysis within Datadog's monitoring platform.

3.Risk: Undetected attacks due to lack of proper log analysis.
3.Measure: Regularly analyze logs using keywords and anomaly detection techniques.

3.Description: Security checks during local development ensure vulnerabilities are identified early. Tools like Git hooks, Pre-commit, and IDE security plugins automate these checks in local environments.

Tools.Name: Git hooks
Tools.Description: Automates security checks during Git operations in local development environments.

Tools.Name: Pre-commit
Tools.Description: Framework for managing multi-language pre-commit hooks to automate local security checks.

Tools.Name: IDE Security Plugins
Tools.Description: Plugins for IDEs like VS Code and IntelliJ IDEA provide real-time security analysis.

3.Risk: Code developed locally may contain vulnerabilities or poor practices.
