3.Measure: Integrate quality and linting plugins in IDEs and enforce pre-commit hooks to prevent insecure code commits.
3.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

3.Activity: Conduction of Build-It, Break-It, Fix-It Contests
3.Description: Organizing contests where teams build, attack, and secure applications promotes hands-on learning and enhances security skills. These events foster collaboration and awareness of secure development practices.
3.Risk: Training often focuses on breaking applications rather than building secure components.
3.Measure: Build-it, break-it, fix-it contests help train security champions and developers to build, attack, and secure applications effectively.

3.Description: Dedicated office hours provide a platform for teams to consult security experts, fostering an open and supportive culture around security concerns.
3.Risk: Lack of communication with security teams may lead to overlooking potential threats during implementation.
3.Measure: Security teams should establish defined office hours to address questions and provide guidance.

3.Description: Testing sequential operations ensures that multi-step workflows in the application execute securely and correctly. Tools like Selenium, Cypress, and JUnit automate the validation of these workflows in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Selenium
Tools.Description: Automates testing of web applications, including sequential workflows.

Tools.Name: Cypress
Tools.Description: End-to-end testing framework for sequential operation validation.

Tools.Name: JUnit
Tools.Description: Java testing framework for validating sequential workflows.

3.Risk: Improper execution of sequential operations can compromise workflows (e.g., login, shopping cart).
3.Measure: Define sequential operations and validate them using automated tests.

3.Activity: Conduction of Simple Threat Modeling on Business Level
3.Description: Simple threat modeling identifies security risks related to business functionalities during the product backlog creation, enabling early mitigation of risks.

Tools.Name: OWASP Threat Dragon
Tools.Description: Collaborative open-source tool for identifying business-level security risks.

Tools.Name: Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool
Tools.Description: Provides templates and guidance for conducting business-level threat modeling.

3.Description: Substantial implementation of advanced security measures, including strong authentication, robust authorization, and secure data storage. Tools like OWASP ZAP, Fortify, and Snyk assist in automating these practices within CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: OWASP ZAP
Tools.Description: Identifies advanced vulnerabilities during CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Fortify
Tools.Description: Performs static and dynamic analysis for advanced security hardening.

Tools.Name: Snyk
Tools.Description: Scans for vulnerabilities in dependencies and container images.

3.Risk: Insecure applications may result in data theft or unauthorized modifications.
3.Measure: Adopt OWASP ASVS Level 2 and implement 75% of the recommendations.
3.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

3.Description: Setting up a basic Web Application Firewall (WAF) configuration provides essential protection against common threats. Tools like AWS WAF, Cloudflare WAF, and ModSecurity automate baseline WAF rule deployments.

Tools.Name: AWS WAF
Tools.Description: Provides baseline security rules integrated with AWS CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Cloudflare WAF
Tools.Description: Offers automated baseline WAF protection for web applications.

Tools.Name: ModSecurity
Tools.Description: Open-source WAF providing baseline protection rules.

3.Risk: Unprotected inputs may allow exploits to infiltrate applications.
3.Measure: Deploy a WAF with baseline rules to balance false positives and improve input security.
3.ISO 27001:2022: Hardening is not explicitly covered by ISO 27001

3.Description: Rolling updates deploy application changes incrementally across servers or instances, minimizing downtime. Tools like Kubernetes, Terraform, and Jenkins automate rolling updates in CI/CD pipelines for reliable deployments.

Tools.Name: Kubernetes
Tools.Description: Automates rolling updates for seamless application deployment.

Tools.Name: Terraform
Tools.Description: Automates incremental infrastructure changes for rolling updates.

Tools.Name: Jenkins
Tools.Description: Orchestrates rolling updates via pipeline scripts.

3.Risk: Deployment without downtime may fail if the application becomes inaccessible during updates.
3.Measure: Implement deployment processes that allow continuous application availability.

3.Description: 'Lessons learned' sessions analyze security incidents to identify root causes and define preventive measures, fostering continuous improvement and enhanced incident response.

Tools.Name: Confluence
Tools.Description: Collaboration tool for documenting and sharing post-incident lessons.

Tools.Name: Root Cause Analysis Tools
Tools.Description: Helps in identifying the underlying causes of incidents.

3.Risk: Failure to analyze security incidents may result in recurring vulnerabilities.
3.Measure: Conduct structured post-incident reviews to identify and mitigate root causes.

3.Description: Testing for unauthorized installations ensures that no unapproved software or applications are deployed within the infrastructure. This reduces risks associated with malicious or unverified software compromising system security. Tools such as Tripwire, OSSEC, and Sysdig can be integrated into DevSecOps pipelines to automate the detection, alerting, and prevention of unauthorized installations.

Tools.Name: Tripwire
Tools.Description: A proprietary solution that monitors and detects unauthorized changes to system files and configurations, integrated into CI/CD pipelines for continuous security.

Tools.Name: OSSEC
Tools.Description: An open-source host-based intrusion detection system that monitors system changes and detects unauthorized installations or configurations, compatible with CI/CD workflows.

Tools.Name: Sysdig
Tools.Description: A runtime security tool providing monitoring and detection of unauthorized installations and activities within containerized environments, integrated into CI/CD pipelines.

3.Risk: Unauthorized components may introduce vulnerabilities, backdoors, or malicious activities.
3.Measure: Enforce strict whitelisting of approved components. Perform regular automated scans on infrastructure, such as Docker clusters, to validate the use of standardized base images.

3.Description: Maintaining an inventory of production dependencies involves tracking third-party libraries, frameworks, and services in use. This ensures that dependencies are secure, updated, and compliant with organizational standards. Tools like Dependabot, Renovate, and Snyk automate dependency tracking and management in CI/CD pipelines.

Tools.Name: Dependabot
Tools.Description: Automates dependency updates by creating pull requests to ensure the production environment is secure.

Tools.Name: Renovate
Tools.Description: Provides automated dependency updates across languages, reducing manual efforts and ensuring security.

Tools.Name: Snyk
Tools.Description: Scans for vulnerabilities in dependencies and monitors their health within CI/CD pipelines.

3.Risk: Delayed identification of vulnerabilities in dependencies increases the risk of exploitation in production.
3.Measure: Document dependencies used in production artifacts like container images, ensuring regular updates and monitoring.

3.Description: Generating response statistics involves collecting and analyzing metrics related to security incident responses, such as Mean Time to Resolution (MTTR). This helps evaluate the effectiveness of incident management processes. Tools like Prometheus and Grafana automate data collection and visualization for informed decision-making.

Tools.Name: Prometheus
Tools.Description: A monitoring system and time series database to collect and store response metrics.

Tools.Name: Grafana
Tools.Description: Visualizes response metrics in customizable dashboards to aid in monitoring and analysis.

3.Risk: Delayed reactions to findings increase the likelihood of successful exploitation of identified vulnerabilities.
3.Measure: Create and maintain response statistics such as MTTR to continuously improve incident response effectiveness.
